news.headline: BlackRock holds 567K+ BTC worth $47B, with $11.6T AUM. Recent BTC acquisition of 268 BTC valued $22M on Mar 14.持BTC: 567,000+,值$47B。最近268BTC交易值$22M。BlackRock率持者，占比全球最大。
news.summary: BlackRock, the world's largest asset manager with $11.6 trillion in assets, holds over 567,000 Bitcoin valued at $47.8 billion. Their most recent BTC acquisition was 268 BTC worth $22 million. They also hold over 1.2 million Ether, 70 million USDC, and various altcoins. BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin fund had $193 million in outflows on March 9 and saw $756 million in outflows for BTC ETFs this month. Despite market uncertainty, BlackRock added the iShares Bitcoin ETF to its model portfolio in February 2025. Model portfolios are preset investment plans promoted to investors by asset managers, driving capital inflows for included assets like the IBIT ETF.

news.headline: Bitcoin and stablecoin growth may speed up dedollarization efforts amid concerns over US dollar dominance. Countries look to shift from dollar dependency. Countries like Russia are using cryptocurrencies to bypass sanctions. While digital assets gain traction, it's too early to see them replacing the US dollar as a global reserve currency. Bitcoin's role as a dollar alternative remains uncertain. Stablecoins, mostly pegged to USD, could reinforce dollar dominance rather than challenge it. Global FX reserves in US dollars have declined, indicating a potential shift away from dollar reliance. Despite efforts to counter US dollar dominance, its value remains strong. Stablecoin market cap at $233 billion, dominated by USD-pegged stablecoins. The emergence of CBDCs may impact stablecoins but the US dollar's dominance as the global reserve currency is unmatched.
news.summary: The US dollar has long been the dominant global reserve currency, accounting for nearly 60% of global foreign exchange reserves. However, its position is increasingly under pressure due to geopolitical and economic shifts, leading to discussions around "dedollarization" and the potential rise of alternatives like Bitcoin. While some countries are exploring ways to reduce their dependence on the dollar, stablecoins and central bank digital currencies may actually reinforce dollar dominance. Despite the potential of cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin to challenge the dollar, experts caution that it is still too early to consider it a true alternative. The US dollar's share of global foreign reserves has declined from over 70% in the early 2000s to below 60%. However, the dollar's strength lies in its stability, widespread acceptance, and status as a key reserve asset for central banks. As discussions around the future of global currencies continue, the US dollar's dominance remains unchallenged, with no viable alternative ready to displace it at present.

Crypto influencer, TJ Stone, sentenced to 45 months in prison for wire fraud, ordered to pay $1.3M. Cases of crypto-related crimes continue to be pursued in New York district court. Prominent figures like SafeMoon's former CEO face criminal charges. Comedian Larry David's ties to FTX advert highlighted. Trend of seeking pardon from Trump reported in crypto criminal cases.
news.summary: Thomas John Sfraga, also known as “TJ Stone,” was sentenced to 45 months in prison and ordered to pay over $1.3 million in forfeiture for wire fraud in a scheme targeting crypto investors. Sfraga convinced victims to invest in a fake cryptocurrency wallet with promises of high returns up to 60% in three months. He used the money for personal expenses and to pay earlier victims. His case is one of many crypto-related crimes pursued in the Eastern District of New York. The cryptocurrency industry has had other connections to popular culture, as seen with a Super Bowl ad featuring Larry David and his loss in a defunct crypto exchange. High-profile figures in cryptocurrency criminal cases, including former FTX CEO Sam Bankman-Fried and former Binance CEO Changpeng Zhao, have reportedly been seeking pardons from President Trump.

news.headline: Can Manufacturers Institute warns steel/aluminum tariffs will inflate costs, harm competitiveness; Canada supplies 2/3 of US aluminum imports.
news.summary: The Can Manufacturers Institute (CMI) has expressed concerns regarding the consequences of recently imposed steel and aluminum tariffs. The CMI cautioned that these tariffs could lead to inflation within the domestic can industry. The Trump administration's rationale behind these tariffs is to safeguard domestic production and lessen dependence on foreign metals. However, manufacturers are apprehensive, believing that the tariffs will escalate expenses and dent competitiveness. CMI President Robet Budway stressed their support for President Donald Trump's actions against unfair trade practices but emphasized that the tariffs will elevate prices for canned goods and beverages. According to the Aluminum Association, Canada supplies almost two-thirds of domestically used aluminum, with Mexico being the third-largest provider. The new tariffs encompass all imports not compliant with the USMCA, including compliant products from Canada and Mexico, which will also be affected starting April 2. Separately, China is subject to a 20% tariff.

news.headline: Stocks surged on Wall Street, S&P 500 up 1.9% for strongest rally since Nov 6, 2024, as shutdown fears ease.
news.summary: Stocks surged on Friday after Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer decided against vetoing a Republican spending bill, calming fears of a government shutdown. The S&P 500 rose by 1.9%, on track for its largest single-day gain since November 6, 2024, when investors flocked to equities post-Trump's election win. Tech stocks and small caps also saw increases of 2.3%, while the Dow Jones rose by 1.5%. Despite this, the market was still on a downward trend for the week, with the S&P 500 set for its fourth consecutive weekly decline, the lengthiest since May 2022. All S&P 500 sectors were up on Friday, but only utilities and energy sectors were set to end the week positively.

March consumer confidence survey by University of Michigan shows an 11% monthly drop and 22% decline from Dec 2024, with inflation expectations up to 4.9%. Economists warn of economic trouble for the US. Republicans, Democrats, and Independents all reported declines in economic expectations. Gold ETFs are on the rise with spot price exceeding $3,000 amidst safe-haven demand. economists express concerns over the survey results.
news.summary: The March consumer confidence survey from the University of Michigan revealed a sharp decline in consumer confidence, alongside a notable surge in inflation expectations, posing concerns for the US economy. The report, unveiled on Friday, indicated an 11% decrease compared to the previous month and a substantial 22% drop since December 2024. Inflation expectations notably rose from 4.3% in February to 4.9% in March, marking the highest level recorded since November 2022. The survey highlighted a degree of political polarization, with all Republicans, Democrats, and Independents expressing reduced economic expectations. Additionally, amid safe-haven demand, Gold ETFs saw a surge as the spot price surpassed $3,000. Economists reacted strongly to the survey findings, labeling them as a significant warning sign for the economy.

news.headline: Europe approves Bristol Myers' Breyanzi cell therapy for patients with relapsed or refractory follicular lymphoma.
news.summary: The European Commission has granted approval to Bristol Myers Squibb & Co‘s Breyanzi (lisocabtagene maraleucel; liso-cel), a CD19-directed chimeric antigen receptor (CAR) T cell therapy. This approval is specifically for adult patients with relapsed or refractory follicular lymphoma (FL) who have undergone two or more lines of systemic therapy. The decision was supported by data from the global Phase 2 TRANSCEND FL study. For more details, the complete story can be found on Benzinga.com.
